name: Navaratna Gems
description: The Navaratna (nine gems) hold immense significance in Hindu astrology and tradition. They are believed to possess cosmic energies that influence human life and are associated with the nine planets (Navagraha). The traditional arrangement includes Ruby (Surya/Sun), Pearl (Chandra/Moon), Red Coral (Mangala/Mars), Emerald (Budha/Mercury), Yellow Sapphire (Guru/Jupiter), Diamond (Shukra/Venus), Blue Sapphire (Shani/Saturn), Hessonite Garnet (Rahu/North Lunar Node), and Cat's Eye Chrysoberyl (Ketu/South Lunar Node). Wearing Navaratna jewelry or possessing these gems is thought to bring good fortune, health, prosperity, and protection from negative influences. The arrangement and quality of the gems are crucial to harness their beneficial effects. The Navaratna's influence extends beyond personal well-being, often used in temple rituals, deity adornments, and as offerings to appease the planets. These gems are more than just beautiful ornaments; they are powerful conduits of cosmic energy, capable of shaping destinies and influencing the balance of the universe. The power of the Navaratna stems from the inherent properties of each gem and their corresponding planetary alignment, creating a harmonious blend of energies that work in unison to bring positive outcomes.
subtitle: Nine gems, cosmic energies, balanced destinies.
origin: Said to have originated from the churning of the cosmic ocean (Samudra Manthan) or as gifts from the deities.
currentLocation: Worn as jewelry, displayed in temples, or held as personal possessions globally.

keyMyths.title: Planetary Influence
keyMyths.description: Each gem corresponds to a specific planet in Hindu astrology, channeling its energy and mitigating negative planetary effects. Wearing the Navaratna is believed to harmonize planetary influences on an individual's life.

keyMyths.title: Samudra Manthan
keyMyths.description: Some believe the gems originated during the churning of the cosmic ocean, making them divine in origin. This event is a central myth in Hindu cosmology, linking the gems to the creation and sustenance of the universe.

sources.author: P.V. Kane
sources.description: Comprehensive study of Hindu religious law and customs.
sources.text: Comprehensive study of Hindu religious law and customs.
